text: Last class I replied to a customer and offered a clear solution.
Today the supplier is silent, and I want to chase a clear answer.
Reminder
Pending
Awaiting
Response
Chase
Urgent
Overdue
Kindly
I am still waiting for your reply.
This is a friendly reminder about my last email.
Could you reply by Friday, please?
We kindly ask for a prompt response.
Hi Karen, the supplier is silent. I wrote a reminder. I open with: This is a friendly reminder about my last email. Can you check it?
Good start. Do you show that you are still waiting? And remember, the matter is still pending on their side.
Yes. I wrote: I am still awaiting your final numbers. The report is now overdue, and this request is urgent.
Perfect, but keep it kind. Set a clear day, and ask politely. Do not sound angry. What did you write?
I wrote: We kindly ask for a prompt response. Could you reply by Friday at the latest?
That is clear and kind. You showed you are waiting, you set a deadline, and you stayed polite. Add me in CC and send it. Great work, Percival.
I am still waiting for your response.
The report is now overdue and urgent.
Could you reply by Friday at the latest?
We kindly ask for a prompt response. Thank you.

Hi Percival, this is Mr. Santos from the supplier. I am very sorry. I just saw your two emails about the contract, and I realize I never replied. It was my mistake. The matter is still pending on my side, but it is now urgent for me too. Your request is completely clear, and I am sorry you had to send a reminder. I am working on it today, and you have my word: you will have my full response by tomorrow morning. Thank you for being patient, and again, I apologize for the silence. Please do not worry. We will not miss the deadline. Have a good day.

Hi Percival, this is Karen. I see the supplier is still awaiting our final numbers, and the report is now overdue. Could you chase this for me, please? Send them a short, polite reminder. Keep it kind, but be clear. Remind them that we are still waiting for their answer, that the request is urgent, and ask them to reply by Friday at the latest. Do not sound angry. Just say we kindly ask for a prompt response. If they do not reply today, we will call them on Monday. Thank you so much, and let me know when it is done.

First, Percival sends a clear request to the supplier and asks for an answer by Friday. Then, Friday passes and no response arrives. After that, he writes a short, polite reminder. Later, he says he is still waiting and kindly asks for a prompt reply. Finally, the supplier apologizes and promises a full response by the next morning.

This is a friendly reminder about my last email.
I am still waiting for your response.
The request is still pending on their side.
Could you reply by Friday, please?
The report is now two days overdue.
We kindly ask for a prompt response.
The report is now overdue and urgent.
Could you reply by Friday at the latest?
We kindly ask for a prompt response. Thank you.
